A small village was blessed with a magical fountain, and the villagers used it to quench their thirst and nourish their crops. One day, a young boy, curious and adventurous, decided to drink from the water after sunset, when the magic would be at its strongest. As he sipped, he felt the power of the water fill him, and he became the protector of the village, watching over the people and the magical fountain, ensuring that its power would never be used for evil.

The Wind and the Willow

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a young girl named Lila. Lila was kind, and one day, she found an injured bird on the ground. She carefully tended to it, and to her surprise, it was a rare and beautiful red-crested parrot. Lila decided to take care of the parrot, and she eventually learned how to speak its language, and the two became close friends.

Once upon a time, in a land far, far away, there was a magical forest. This forest was filled with talking animals, and the most respected and wise of them all was a tree called Gnome. Gnome, a wise and ancient creature, provided wisdom and direction to the other, less- wise, trees, and, of course, the animals.

Once upon a time, in a far away land, there was a small, but mighty, kingdom. The king, wise and fair, used his power to protect and serve the people. One day, a young, brave, and kind knight, named Sir Lancel, came to the Kingdom, to prove his courage with a series of challenges. The King, seeing the young knight, decided to give a task: to find the legendary "Sword of Truth", to ensure that the King’s reign would continue, and the people would be protected.

Once, in the land of Narnia, a lion named Aslan brought a human, Edmund, back to his home. Before leaving, Aslan told Edmund, "In Narnia, we all learn from our mistakes, and when you go back, you will be different, with more strength and understanding, which is a very good thing, not just in Narnia, but to take into your whole life, and when you grow, for you, and for those around you.

The little caterpillar

One day, a little caterpillar, named Catt, decided to go on an adventure. Catt left his leaf, and walked along the forest floor, enjoying the view of different flora. Finally, he reached the top of a tree, and as he was climbing, he found a beautiful, large, white, snowdrop. 

Catt, being so excited, started to dance, and as he started, he began to transform, and became a beautiful, small, white, Snowdrop, just like he was looking at. Once upon a time, in a forest deep with a dense fog, a young tree was found by the wind. The wind could not help but marvel at the young tree, its beauty, and the way it seemed to stand so tall, even in the midst of such uncertainty. The wind asked the young tree, "Why do you stand so tall, even when there is such fog and uncertainty around?"  
The young tree replied, "I stand tall because I have faith.  
There is fog, but in my heart, I can see the sun.  
There is uncertainty, but in my heart, I can feel the wind.  
I stand tall, for even though the world is not as I know,  
I still hold on to all that I have,  
And I still believe,  
Because,  
In my heart,  
I can feel,  
And,  
In my heart,  
I can see,  
And,  
In my heart,  
I can hear,

Once there was a wizard, named Torm, who had a magical hat. This hat could turn any object into an ordinary, non-magical one, or it could turn an ordinary, non-magical object into a magical one. Torm used this hat to help people, making their wishes come true, and he made himself rich, by using the same hat.

Once upon a time, in a small village, there was a wise old man. The villagers, including the children, would listen to his stories and advice, as he had a unique way of explaining things. One day, he told a story about a young boy, who, after hearing from the wise old man, went on a journey, and finally returned, changed.
